{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "a-maccormack", "type": "analyst"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "analyst", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/78695941?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/a-maccormack", "id": 78695941, "login": "a-maccormack", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjc4Njk1OTQx", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/a-maccormack", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48793", "cvss": {"score": 8.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": ["CWE-88"], "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-88", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Argument Delimiters in a Command ('Argument Injection')"}], "description": "### Impact
A potential FFmpeg argument injection vulnerability exists in the subtitle conversion code path, of the same vulnerability class as [CVE-2023-49096 ](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/cve-2023-49096) and [CVE-2025-31499](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-31499), in a code path that was not addressed by either fix.

`SubtitleEncoder.ConvertTextSubtitleToSrtInternal` (SubtitleEncoder.cs, line 382) interpolates the subtitle file path into FFmpeg command-line arguments without calling `EncodingUtils.NormalizePath()`. On Linux, filenames can contain double-quote characters, which break the argument quoting and allow injection of arbitrary FFmpeg arguments. The vulnerability is reachable without authentication via `SubtitleController.GetSubtitle`, which has no `[Authorize]` attribute. An attacker who can place a file in a Jellyfin media library directory (shared NAS, Samba share, guest upload) can achieve arbitrary file write on the server and information disclosure.

### Patches
This issue has been fixed in Jellyfin 10.11.10.

### Workarounds
N/A

### References

Reported by Alister MacCormack (@a-maccormack).

title: Potential FFmpeg argument injection via unescaped subtitle file path
